title: Consider making Podman version info compatible with tools designed for Docker
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

3rd party tools can query `docker info` and expect a particular docker engine version, e.g. [`20.10.17 or higher version`](https://github.com/nhost/cli/blob/2097507c4223386b02890a6bba4b4eec86537e7c/nhost/service/launcher.go#L91).

For the Podman CLI to be more compatible with Docker CLI there would need to be an equivalent mapping to Docker engine version (even if only emulated) so that tools that are intended to work with Docker might work with Podman out of the box, and to avoid potential compatibility not to even be realized because of irrelevant checks such as this.

E.g. Podman versions (real) can be seperated from Docker versions (emulated) but tools would first check the emulated versions, unless they explicitly point to the real podman versions.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman and podman-docker

2. `docker info`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.

...

version:
  APIVersion: 4.3.1
  PodmanAPIVersion: 4.3.1
  Built: 1668178887
  BuiltTime: Fri Nov 11 17:01:27 2022
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.19.2
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 4.3.1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Implementation might not be exactly like this. But in some way, a default for podman naive 3rd party applications to get similar data as if it was an equivalent version of actual Docker... with a way for podman aware 3rd party applications to get podman specific data.

```
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.

...
emulated:
  Version: 20.10.21
  APIVersion: 20.10.21
version:
  APIVersion: 4.3.1
  Built: 1668178887
  BuiltTime: Fri Nov 11 17:01:27 2022
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.19.2
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 4.3.1
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.3.1
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Note, [this comment](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11227#issuecomment-902058979) seems to be describing exactly what I am proposing. If it is meant to be like this already, then I think there is a bug

This might be useful to show what `docker info` looks like when running against host's podman socket:

1. `alias docker="podman run --privileged -it -v /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock docker:dind docker $@"`

2. `docker info`

```

  compose: Docker Compose (Docker Inc., v2.13.0)

WARNING: bridge-nf-call-ip6tables is disabled

The comment you link is about the API returned information, I believe.  You are asking for the Podman CLI to show related docker versions.

Do you know of a specific tool tha relies on the docker client to check its version?

If so I would expect the tool to be doing something like

$ docker version --format '{{ json .}}'
{"Client":{"Platform":{"Name":"Docker Engine - Community"},"Version":"20.10.21","ApiVersion":"1.41","DefaultAPIVersion":"1.41","GitCommit":"baeda1f","GoVersion":"go1.18.7","Os":"linux","Arch":"amd64","BuildTime":"Tue Oct 25 18:02:36 2022","Context":"default","Experimental":true},"Server":null}

$ podman version --format '{{ json .}}'
{"Client":{"APIVersion":"4.3.1","Version":"4.3.1","GoVersion":"go1.19.2","GitCommit":"","BuiltTime":"Fri Nov 11 10:01:27 2022","Built":1668178887,"OsArch":"linux/amd64","Os":"linux"}}

Adding  something like this to the Podman output, especially if the ARG0=="docker", might be possible.

{"Platform":{"Name":"Docker Engine - Community"},"Version":"20.10.21","ApiVersion":"1.41","DefaultAPIVersion":"1.41","GitCommit":"baeda1f","GoVersion":"go1.18.7","Os":"linux","Arch":"amd64","BuildTime":"Tue Oct 25 18:02:36 2022","Context":"default","Experimental":true},"Server":null}

But knowing tools that verify this would help make your agurment.

WDYT @vrothberg @mheon @baude

I added a hyperlink to a specific example. It is done using Go. Not so sure if that is what you are looking for.

Repeated for convenience - https://github.com/nhost/cli/blob/2097507c4223386b02890a6bba4b4eec86537e7c/nhost/service/launcher.go#L91 (i.e. it reads from attribute info.ServerVersion which I believe comes via the [Go client for the Docker Engine API](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/docker/docker/client#section-readme))
Is the cleint program talking to the docker daemon or just execing the docker client?

It connects to the docker (in this case, Podman) unix socket defined by
environment variable `DOCKER_HOST`

On Sun, 4 Dec 2022, 23:03 Daniel J Walsh, ***@***.***> wrote:

> Is the cleint program talking to the docker daemon or just execing the
> docker client?
>
> —

> .
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.Message ID:
> ***@***.***>
>

I sympathize with the argument.  "Vanilla" Podman is clearly not Docker-compatible on the `info` command.  That's on purpose but I understand that `podman-docker` should be as close to Docker as possible.

My solution is now to dual boot an Ubuntu along with my Fedora. Podman
comes very close to being a drop in replacement for docker, I was able to
work at my previous company with only podman.

However this is an example of an edge case, where the product (3rd party,
nhost) would need to directly support podman for it to work.

On Mon, 5 Dec 2022, 12:07 Valentin Rothberg, ***@***.***>
wrote:

> I sympathize with the argument. "Vanilla" Podman is clearly not
> Docker-compatible on the info command. That's on purpose but I understand
> that podman-docker should be as close to Docker as possible.
>

> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.Message ID:
> ***@***.***>
>

Well if the API is a problem, then we should think about fixing it. I think we are returning the Podman version on the API, where the caller obviously expects us to return the Docker Daemon version so it can verify the API is new enough to support it's calls.  @mheon @jwhonce @baude WDYT?  Should we return the Docker Daemon version?

> Do you know of a specific tool that relies on the docker client to check its version?

Encountered this with Scala build tool plugin for packaging container apps. Examples:
https://github.com/sbt/sbt-native-packager/blob/68600dfb60327aceb619cb9138d3c8f6e8504510/src/main/scala/com/typesafe/sbt/packager/docker/DockerPlugin.scala#L145
https://github.com/sbt/sbt-native-packager/blob/68600dfb60327aceb619cb9138d3c8f6e8504510/src/main/scala/com/typesafe/sbt/packager/docker/DockerPlugin.scala#L150

Tool provide means to force needed behavior using flags, but this was not the best experience, so, I even switched to good old terminal.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Ok looking at this further we are returning the correct information for APIversion, but not what certain tools expect for Version.  But I don't think it would be appropriate to change the version of the server to some fake docker version.

So I am going to close this Issue. as won't fix.
